summary: Comprehensive diagnostic matrix covering five distinct Linux kernel lockup scenarios: 1) Hard lockup with timer discrepancy and spinlock deadlock, 2) Hard lockup due to spinlock with NULL owner, 3) Soft/hard lockup from ring_buffer race condition, 4) Hard lockup from qrwlock deadlock with Mellanox driver, 5) Hard/soft lockup from massive negative dentries causing shrink_slab contention.

matrix.trigger: Watchdog detected hard LOCKUP with timer_jiffies discrepancy
matrix.arg_hints: cpu: from watchdog message or bt -a
matrix.why: Identify which CPU is locked up and examine its stack
matrix.expect: Stack showing _spin_lock_irqsave or similar lock function

matrix.arg_hints: addr: from RAX/RBX register in stack trace
matrix.why: Check spinlock state (ticket values, owner)
Find CPU holding lock in idle state
Verify preempt_count should be non-zero for lock holder

matrix.action: p tvec_bases:a | awk '{print "struct tvec_base.timer_jiffies " $9}' | while read cmd; do crash -q -c "$cmd" 2>/dev/null; done
matrix.why: Check timer_jiffies vs current jiffies across all CPUs

matrix.trigger: Watchdog threads CPU affinity issue suspected
matrix.why: Check if watchdog threads have proper CPU affinity
Check for tasks stuck in sync_page, io_schedule
matrix.expect: Tasks in UN state with sync_page/io_schedule in stack

matrix.why: Check for stuck SCSI commands
matrix.expect: Active scsi_cmnd with negative deadline values

matrix.why: Identify non-standard modules that may interfere

matrix.trigger: Panic without hard lockup message but watchdog_overflow_callback in stack
matrix.arg_hints: rip_addr: exception RIP address from panic
matrix.why: Examine assembly to find lock address in RBX/RDI
matrix.expect: MOV instruction loading lock address into RBX

matrix.why: Check spinlock state with owner field

matrix.action: bt -a | grep exception\\ RIP -A3 | grep spin -A3 | grep -oE 'RBX: ffff.{12}' | uniq -c
matrix.why: Find all CPUs waiting on same lock
matrix.expect: Multiple CPUs with same RBX lock address

matrix.trigger: Multiple CPUs waiting on same lock
matrix.why: Check for expired timers due to interrupt disable
matrix.expect: Negative expiration times on affected CPUs

matrix.trigger: CPU stuck in perf_event_wakeup or ring_buffer_wakeup
matrix.arg_hints: rb_addr: from R12 register or stack
matrix.why: Check ring_buffer event_list for corruption
matrix.expect: event_list.next/prev pointing to self or invalid

matrix.arg_hints: event_addr: from RAX in list_for_each_entry_rcu loop
matrix.why: Check perf_event rb_entry linkage
matrix.expect: rb_entry not properly linked to ring_buffer

matrix.why: Examine list_for_each_entry_rcu loop assembly

matrix.trigger: Hard lockup with queued_write_lock_slowpath in stack
matrix.why: Identify qrwlock address
Check qrwlock cnts and lock fields
Detailed qrwlock state analysis
Examine why reader holds lock indefinitely
matrix.expect: Task in do_wait with mlx5_irq_int_handler in IRQ

matrix.why: Check for tainted out-of-tree modules
matrix.expect: mlx5_core, mlx5_ib modules with OE taint

matrix.why: Check CPU scheduling delays
matrix.expect: CPU showing high secs lag (e.g., 18821.87 secs)

matrix.trigger: Hard lockup with native_queued_spin_lock_slowpath and rcu_sched stall
matrix.why: Identify which rcu_node lock is contended
matrix.expect: RDI register showing rcu_node lock address

matrix.why: Check lock contention state
Check dcache_lru_lock state
matrix.expect: Counter value like 0x3090000 (contended but not locked)

matrix.why: Check slab cache memory usage
matrix.expect: SLAB cache using high percentage of total RAM (e.g., 79%)

matrix.action: kmem -s | awk 'NR>1{printf($1 "\t" $NF "\t %8.3f MiB\n", $2*$4/2**20)}' | sort -k3nr | column -t | head
matrix.why: Identify largest slab caches
matrix.expect: dentry cache using hundreds of GB

matrix.why: Check dentry statistics
Check if negative dentry limit is set
Timer discrepancy may be caused by watchdog affinity issues
Check if timer interrupts are frozen on affected CPUs
matrix.expect: hrtimer_interrupts unchanged for CPUs waiting on lock

matrix.trigger: Ring_buffer event_list corrupted with perf_event_wakeup loop
matrix.why: Check wait queue state

matrix.trigger: qrwlock deadlock with Mellanox driver in IRQ
matrix.why: Check if swapper also affected by driver IRQ
